items.title: Expectation as center of mass
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  dist["Distribution over values of X"]
  mu["E[X] = balance point"]
  spread["Var(X) = spread around μ"]
  dist --> mu
  mu --> spread
items.caption: E[X] locates the probability-weighted center; Var(X) measures how far values typically fall from that center.

items.title: Linearity of expectation vs variance of a sum
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  sum["X + Y"]
  sum --> e_lin["E[X+Y] = E[X] + E[Y] always"]
  sum --> v_dep["Var(X+Y) = Var(X) + Var(Y) + 2Cov(X,Y)"]
  v_dep --> indep["If independent: Cov = 0, variances add"]
items.caption: Expectation is linear in sums without extra terms; variance of a sum generally needs a covariance correction unless variables are independent.

items.title: From population to sample to ML metrics
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  pop["E[X], Var(X)"]
  sample["x̄, s² from data"]
  ml["MSE, RMSE, MAE on predictions"]
  pop --> sample
  sample --> ml
items.caption: Population moments define theoretical averages and spread; sample statistics estimate them; training and evaluation losses summarize expected prediction errors.
